title: Protocols for mRNA electroporation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eof3a","text":"This protocol we have used for the electroporation of mRNA encoding reporter gene Lucipherase into following organisms: Chromera velia, Alexandrium minutum, Euglena gracilis, Pyramimonas parkease, Pyramimonas orientalis, Eutreptiella gymnastica, Pseudonitzschia multiseries and Trichomonas vaginalis (a control). In any case we have not detected a specific lucipharease activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":120,"length":14},{"style":"italic","offset":136,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":157,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":175,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":197,"length":77},{"style":"italic","offset":278,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare luciferase mRNA using mMESSAGE mMACHINE® T7 Ultra Kit.
Check the quality of mRNA by rabbit reticulocyte lysate translation and measurement of luciferase activity.
Prepare cells for electroporation to the final concentration 1x107cells/ml.
Place 4mm electroporation cuvettes on ice and pipet 300 µl of cells.
Add 1-5 µl of mRNA and mix well by pipetting.
After electroporation immediately place cells into the fresh media.
After 6-12-18-24 hours take samples for measurement of luciferase activity.
Break cells by beatbeater: 75-150 µm glass beads, 4800g (max), 1 min
Transfer 30 µl of supernatant to measuring tube. Use 30 µl of lysis buffer as blank sample.
Add 100 µl luciferin and measure activity immediately.
creator: Vladimir Hampl
